nodes.title: Vectors & Matrices — Core Operations
nodes.fsd_analogy: A matrix is a typed lookup table — rows are records, columns are fields, multiplication is a relational join with computed projections

nodes.title: Calculus — Derivatives & Gradients
nodes.fsd_analogy: A gradient is like a profiler flame graph — it tells you which weights (hotspots) contribute most to your loss (slow render), so you know where to optimize first

nodes.title: Distributions & Bayesian Statistics
nodes.fsd_analogy: Bayes theorem is a confidence score updater — like updating a feature-flag rollout percentage as you collect real-user data

nodes.title: Linear Models — Regression & Classification
nodes.assignment_title: Build regressor from scratch, evaluate metrics
nodes.fsd_analogy: Logistic regression is a single middleware function — takes inputs, applies learned weights, outputs a probability score, just like an auth middleware scoring token validity

nodes.title: Tree-Based Models & Ensembles
nodes.fsd_analogy: Random Forest is a microservices vote — 100 weak services each give an answer, majority wins; XGBoost is sequential — each service patches the previous one's mistakes

nodes.title: Dimensionality Reduction — PCA & Clustering
nodes.fsd_analogy: PCA is like gzip for feature vectors — it compresses high-dimensional data into its most informative axes without losing the signal that matters

nodes.title: Multi-Layer Perceptrons — Forward & Backward Pass
nodes.fsd_analogy: Forward pass is rendering a React component tree; backward pass is React DevTools telling you which component caused the unnecessary re-render (which weight caused the loss)

nodes.title: Optimization — Gradient Descent Variants
nodes.fsd_analogy: Adam optimizer is like an adaptive rate limiter — it tracks recent gradient history to set a custom step size per parameter, like Nginx adjusting per-route timeouts based on traffic patterns

nodes.title: Attention Mechanisms — Self-Attention
nodes.fsd_analogy: Self-attention is a SQL JOIN where the table joins itself — every token queries every other token to compute how much attention it should pay, like a message bus where every subscriber scores relevance

nodes.title: Transformer Architecture — Encoder-Decoder
nodes.fsd_analogy: GPT is a stateless REST API that streams tokens left-to-right like a cursor; BERT is a batch-mode processor that reads the full request before responding
nodes.java_note: minGPT from scratch has no Java equivalent. DJL covers inference but not educational scratch implementations.

nodes.title: Fine-Tuning — LoRA & QLoRA
nodes.fsd_analogy: LoRA is like monkey-patching a library — instead of rewriting the full model (expensive), you inject small adapter matrices that redirect behavior without touching the base weights
nodes.java_note: No Java ecosystem for LoRA/QLoRA fine-tuning. Python-only.

nodes.title: Augmented Generation — RAG & Vector Search
nodes.assignment_title: Build full-stack RAG document QA API
nodes.fsd_analogy: RAG is an API gateway pattern — instead of the LLM hallucinating from memory, it queries a vector DB (specialized microservice) and injects the retrieved context into the prompt

nodes.title: Inference Serving — High-Throughput Engines
nodes.assignment_title: Deploy quantized model via API endpoint
nodes.fsd_analogy: Continuous batching in vLLM is like connection pooling — instead of one request per thread, it dynamically groups in-flight requests to maximize GPU utilization like a DB pool maximizes connection reuse
nodes.java_note: vLLM and TensorRT-LLM are Python/C++ only. Spring AI can call a vLLM endpoint — reference in stack-comparisons.

nodes.title: AI Agents — Orchestration, Tool Use & Memory
nodes.fsd_analogy: ReAct agents are a state machine with an LLM as the transition function — Reason → Act → Observe loops mirror a CI pipeline: plan the step, run the tool, check the output

nodes.title: GPU/TPU Hardware — Interconnect & Memory
nodes.lesson_topic: NVLink, InfiniBand, VRAM constraints, HBM bandwidth
nodes.assignment_title: Profile memory/network bottlenecks during distributed run
nodes.fsd_analogy: NVLink between GPUs is like a high-speed internal LAN — orders of magnitude faster than PCIe (WAN), so keep hot data local; HBM is the L2 cache, DRAM is disk
nodes.java_note: GPU profiling tooling is Python/CUDA only. No Java equivalent.

nodes.title: Distributed Training — Parallelism Strategies
nodes.lesson_topic: Data Parallelism (DDP), Tensor Parallelism (TP), Pipeline Parallelism
nodes.fsd_analogy: Data Parallelism is horizontal scaling (same app, N replicas); Tensor Parallelism is vertical sharding (split one operation across GPUs like DB sharding a single table); Pipeline Parallelism is a microservice pipeline (each GPU handles one model layer)
nodes.java_note: Megatron-LM and DeepSpeed are Python-only. No Java ecosystem for large-scale distributed training.

nodes.title: Cloud FinOps — Cost Metrics & Infrastructure Design
nodes.lesson_topic: Spot instances, serverless inference cost, GPU vs CPU trade-offs
nodes.assignment_title: Build cost-optimization dashboard mapping throughput/dollar on AWS/GCP
nodes.fsd_analogy: Spot instance management for training is like blue-green deployments with auto-eviction — checkpoint every N minutes so a spot reclaim only costs you one checkpoint interval, not the full run
